title: cerastium pumilum var. tetrandrum
intro: Cerastium pumilum var. tetrandrum, commonly known as alpine chickweed or four-leaf stitchwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of cerastium pumilum var. tetrandrum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
